gpu: AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (radeonsi, gfx1201, LLVM 21.1.8, DRM 3.64, 6.18.6-1-default)
notes:
As of January 23, no recent version of Proton will launch the game via Steam. Was working fine  just days before. My workaround was to install the full-fledged EA App via Lutris, install the game via the EA App, and launch from within the EA App. All of my save files were present.

I can also launch the game and the launcher in the Steam folder (.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Mass Effect Legendary Edition/Game/) by just double-clicking the executables, but my save files are missing. Launching the game from within the EA App is currently the only way that I get both a working game and my save files.

notes: I beat all of ME1 and got about 20 hours into ME2 before it refused to launch all together. It would show the "preparing game" screen, the screen would close, and then nothing would happen. I suspect the EA app is the culprit but any and all fixes I've tried have not changed anything so far. And I've tried everything from uninstalling the EA app, repairing the EA app, reinstalling the game (three different times), using protontricks to launch the EA app and login, switching proton versions, none of which did anything to help. It seems that the game is just not going to function anymore
